content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA containing NNN integers. A subsequence of this array is called good if all the elements of this subsequence are distinct.

Find the number of good non-empty subsequences of AAA. This number can be large, so report it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.

Note that two subsequences are considered different if they differ in the indices chosen. For example, in the array [1,1][1, 1][1,1] there are 3 different non-empty subsequences: [1][1][1] (the first element), [1][1][1] (the second element) and [1,1][1, 1][1,1]. Out of these three subsequences, the first two are good and the third is not.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, denoting the number of elements in AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the number of good subsequences modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 1000000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the number of good non‑empty subsequences of the given array, modulo 1000000007.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: counts -> {1:2}. Product = (2+1)=3, minus 1 = 2.\nTest case 2: counts -> {1:1,2:1}. Product = (1+1)*(1+1)=4, minus 1 = 3.

solution.approach: For each distinct value v let cnt[v] be its frequency in the array. In a good subsequence we can take at most one occurrence of v, and if we decide to take it we have cnt[v] choices (which index). Hence for v we have (cnt[v] + 1) possibilities: pick none or pick one of its occurrences. The choices for different values are independent, so the total number of subsequences (including the empty one) equals the product of (cnt[v] + 1) over all distinct values. Subtract 1 to exclude the empty subsequence. Compute the product modulo MOD = 1 000 000 007. The algorithm scans the array, counts frequencies (using a hash map or an array because Ai ≤ 10^6), then multiplies the terms. Complexity per test case is O(N) time and O(D) extra space where D is the number of distinct values.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(D) where D is the number of distinct values in a test case (at most min(N, 10^6)).
